name: tramstec
displayName: TramsTec — WhatsApp infrastructure for AI agents
description: MCP server that lets AI agents operate WhatsApp businesses in Mexico/LATAM. 20 audited tools cover messaging, customer lookup, orders, payments (Stripe, Mercado Pago, SPEI), CFDI 4.0 invoicing, human handoff, consent, and a key-value memory store. Multi-tenant, audited, idempotent. Bring your own LLM (Claude, OpenAI Agents, LangChain, Cursor, n8n, custom).

tiers.name: Developer
tiers.notes: Free. Sandbox environment, MCP docs, test WhatsApp workflows. For developers building and validating an agent.

tiers.name: Production
tiers.notes: Launch real AI agents on WhatsApp. Production WhatsApp infrastructure, context memory, reliable orchestration, agent-to-human handoff, conversation analytics.

tiers.name: Platform
tiers.notes: Scale thousands of conversations. Multi-tenant infrastructure, white-label ready (API-level), advanced observability, API access for platforms and agencies, priority support.

tiers.name: Enterprise
tiers.notes: Custom infrastructure for large-scale AI operations. Dedicated deployment, custom SLAs, security & compliance review, advanced integrations, enterprise onboarding.

pricing.fineprint: Prices listed in USD. Usage-based billing on tool calls, memory operations and active conversations. WhatsApp/Meta fees and payment gateway commissions pass through at cost.

transports.notes: Multi-tenant production. API key issued after sign-up. Scopes supported.

tools.name: whatsapp_send_message
tools.description: Send a WhatsApp text message in an existing conversation. Respects opt-out and 24h window; queued via BullMQ with retry. Idempotent via client_request_id.

tools.name: whatsapp_send_template
tools.description: Send a Meta-approved WhatsApp template message. Requires opt-in. Idempotent.

tools.name: whatsapp_send_media
tools.description: Send image/document/audio/video to a WhatsApp contact. Media URL must be publicly reachable (Meta downloads it). Idempotent.

tools.name: whatsapp_template_submit
tools.description: Submit a WhatsApp template to Meta for approval. Persists in local registry. Admin scope.

tools.name: customer_lookup
tools.description: Look up a contact by customer_id or phone. Returns contact data + recent orders + open conversation.

tools.name: product_search
tools.description: Search the org's product catalog by name, SKU, or description. Uses pgvector for embeddings when OPENAI_API_KEY is configured; falls back to LIKE substring matching.

tools.name: inventory_check
tools.description: Check current stock for one or more SKUs.

tools.name: order_create_draft
tools.description: Create an order in draft state. Computes totals and a summary_for_customer string. Never auto-confirms. Idempotent.

tools.name: order_confirm
tools.description: Confirm a draft order with explicit evidence (customer message or operator action). Idempotent.

tools.name: payment_link_create
tools.description: Generate a checkout link via Stripe or Mercado Pago (or a mock adapter for testing). OXXO Pay supported via Stripe MX. Idempotent.

tools.name: spei_instructions_generate
tools.description: Generate Mexican SPEI bank-transfer instructions with a unique reference. Idempotent.

tools.name: payment_status_check
tools.description: Look up the latest status of a payment. Fetches from the PSP for freshness on non-mock providers.

tools.name: handoff_to_human
tools.description: Escalate a conversation to a human operator with structured reason, urgency, and summary. Disables the AI agent on that conversation.

tools.name: conversation_summarize
tools.description: Build a heuristic structured digest of the last N messages in a conversation. Persists the summary. No LLM call inside.

tools.name: conversation_search
tools.description: Full-text substring search across conversation messages within the org.

tools.name: consent_update
tools.description: Record an opt-in or opt-out event with auditable evidence. LFPDPPP compliant. Idempotent.

tools.name: invoice_create
tools.description: Issue a Mexican CFDI 4.0 invoice via Facturapi. Requires FACTURAPI_KEY. Idempotent per payment_id.

tools.name: memory_set
tools.description: Set a key/value entry in per-contact memory store. Supports TTL. Idempotent.

tools.name: memory_get
tools.description: Read a key from per-contact memory store. Returns {found:false} if missing or expired.

tools.name: memory_list
tools.description: List up to 200 memory entries for a contact.

resources.description: Frequently asked questions for the org.
Customer profile, recent orders, open conversation.

prompts.name: sales_agent_es_mx
prompts.description: Mexico-Spanish sales agent prompt with TramsTec tool hooks.

prompts.name: human_handoff_summary
prompts.description: Conversation summarization optimized for human operators.

prompts.name: template_suggestion_agent
prompts.description: Suggests WhatsApp templates to submit to Meta based on conversation patterns.

compliance: WhatsApp Business Policy (opt-out kill switch, 24h window enforcement)
Mexican LFPDPPP — audit log, opt-out detection (BAJA/STOP/DESUSCRIBIR)
No payment auto-confirmation — only verified PSP webhooks (Stripe HMAC + Meta HMAC) or authenticated human operators can mark payments paid
PCI DSS pass-through via Stripe / Mercado Pago — TramsTec never stores card data
Multi-tenant isolation via application-level org_id assertions on every query
compatibility: n8n / Make / Zapier (via HTTP/SSE manual configuration)
audience: AI engineers building vertical agents on WhatsApp
AI-native SaaS companies needing a WhatsApp execution layer
Agencies operating multiple WhatsApp clients in Mexico/LATAM
LATAM businesses (retail, services, logistics) moving from human-typed replies to agent-with-context

roadmap.intent: Capabilities advertised on the website that are NOT yet in this manifest will land here as they ship. We publish this list to keep marketing and code honest.

items.name: pgvector HNSW index + p95 <50ms semantic memory search
AI extraction tools: extract_purchase_intent, extract_lead_insights, extract_orders_from_conversation
Prompts in en_US, pt_BR, es_AR, es_CO

honest_caveats: Manifest version 0.3.0 (2026-06-16) audit-aligned: the previous 0.2.0 manifest listed 19 tools that do not exist in the runtime. This version lists only the 20 tools actually implemented and callable today.
product_search semantic mode requires OPENAI_API_KEY; without it, embeddings degrade to pseudo-random and the search falls back to LIKE substring matching.
payment_link_create supports stripe, mercadopago, and a mock adapter. conekta and openpay adapters are in the roadmap.
Single MCP server process for HTTP/SSE — session store is in-memory. Multi-instance horizontal scaling requires a Redis-backed session store (roadmap).
BullMQ retry queue is wired with exponential backoff; the worker entrypoint must be started manually in deployment until the auto-start ships.
